new_classes.definition_en: A material entity derived from sugar cane that has undergone fermentation, serving as the feedstock for the distillation of rum and other sugar-cane spirits under CN heading 2208 40.

A distillation process in which fermented sugar-cane products are distilled to produce rum or other sugar-cane spirits classified under CN heading 2208 40, retaining their organoleptic characteristics.

A quality borne by a spirit indicating that the beverage retains the sensory characteristics (aroma, taste, colour) derived from its raw material and production process, as required for classification under CN subheadings 2208 40 11 to 2208 40 99.

new_classes.label_en: rum or other sugar-cane spirit in containers ≤ 2 L (CN 2208 40 51)
new_classes.label_de: Rum oder andere Zuckerrohrbrände in Behältnissen ≤ 2 L (KN 2208 40 51)
new_classes.definition_en: A spirituous beverage obtained by distilling fermented sugar-cane products (including rum and tafia), retaining its organoleptic characteristics, presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres, classified under CN code 2208 40 51.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three main distinguishing criteria from the legal text: (1) the product is a spirit obtained by distilling fermented sugar-cane products (modelled via SugarCaneDistillationProcess with FermentedSugarCaneProduct as feedstock), (2) it retains organoleptic characteristics (modelled via OrganolepticCharacteristicsQuality), and (3) it is in containers ≤ 2 L (modelled via SmallContainer part restriction). The specific mention of 'rum and tafia' as examples is not individually subclassed (they are examples, not exhaustive enumeration). The HS Explanatory Note reference to heading 2208, third paragraph (3) cannot be axiomatised directly as it is a cross-reference to external legal text. These two gaps reduce the score slightly from 1.0.
